name: Xnipec (Tomato-habanero Salsa)
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons fresh sour orange or lime juice
1/2 whole habanero chile, or more to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Chop the onion very finely using a knife (do not use a food processor).
2. Rinse the chopped onion under cold water and shake off excess moisture.
3. Transfer the rinsed onion to a small bowl and stir in the sour orange or lime juice.
4. Set aside.
5. Core the tomatoes, then cut them in half crosswise and squeeze out the seeds.
6. Finely dice the tomatoes into about 1/4 inch cubes.
7. Transfer the diced tomatoes to the same bowl as the onion mixture.
8. Slice radishes to a thickness of 1/16 inch, then chop into matchsticks or small dice.
9. Add the chopped radishes to the tomato mixture.
10. Peel and carefully remove the habanero's seed pod using rubber gloves.
11. Mince the habanero flesh into tiny bits and add it to the tomato mixture.
12. With a very sharp knife, slice the cilantro sprigs to a thickness of 1/16 inch, stems included.
13. Combine the chopped radishes, minced habanero, and sliced cilantro with the tomato mixture.
14. Stir in the onion and juice mixture.
15. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt as needed.
Makes 2 cups of salsa.
This salsa is best enjoyed within a few hours of preparation. The longer it sits, the hotter it will become. You may replace habanero chiles with 3 to 5 jalapenos or serranos, or use 1/2 to 1 Manzano chile.
This Yucatan-style salsa (Xnipec) is excellent as an accompaniment for dishes flavored with achiote. If tomatoes are omitted, the resulting salsa is called salpicon.
